item.description: Fullcourtpass: Giannis Antetokounmpo WILL BE traded before the draft and is focused on Boston or Miami, per @ShamsCharania “I want to make this clear, whichever deal occurs, it’s going to be a one for one deal. It’s going to consist of MIL and one of these two teams. There’s no 3rd and 4th teams.”

item.title: Giannis is content with either of these two …
item.description: ‪Tommy Beer‬: Per Shams: Giannis is expected to be traded before tomorrow’s draft Likely headed to either Boston (who have made Jaylen Brown available) or Miami Giannis is content with either of these two destinations (willing to sign extension)

item.title: Jaylen Brown on people wanting him traded: 'You're turning me into a monster'
item.description: Daniel Donabedian: Jaylen Brown spoke about his social media feed yesterday and how he’s “grateful” for the negativity on it: “That just gives more fuel to the fire…To all the people that’s doubted me, that want me to do this, or want me gone, you’re turning me into a monster.” 🎥 @FCHWPO on Twitch

item.title: Jaylen Brown to be traded regardless of whether Celtics land Giannis Antetokounmpo?
item.description: Heat Central: Brian Windhorst believes Jaylen Brown will be traded even if the Celtics miss on Giannis and is accepting that reality: “I believe if the Bucks choose the Miami Heat offer, Jaylen Brown will get traded somewhere else. They are going to move on here…. From what I understand, Jaylen Brown is accepting this and realizing it’s a chance to turn the page and may be getting his own team”

item.title: As much as he would love to be in Miami, he would be …
item.description: Barry Jackson: I asked Giannis associate why not say Miami and only Miami. Answer was: As much as he would love to be in Miami, he would be OK with Boston too. It was conveyed to at least one (far) West team that he would not extend there, which dissuaded that team. But Giannis camp ultimately was not going to try to dissuade every team.

item.title: Wolves hot for Derrick White?
item.description: Minnesota has been most aggressive in pursuing White, the Celtics’ defensive mastermind who has the experience and the savvy to help Edwards and the Wolves break through in the West. White turns 32 next month and saw his 3-point shooting percentage drop considerably this season, but so far the Celtics have rebuffed the Wolves’ advances.

item.title: Knicks assistant Chris Jent getting real consideration for Blazers' head coaching job
item.description: Marc Stein: Dallas’ hiring of Dusty May leaves Portland as the NBA’s only team without a head coach entering Tuesday’s draft … with sources saying Knicks assistant Chris Jent is getting real consideration for that post alongside finalists Micah Nori (Wolves) and Tyler Lashbrook (Celtics).

item.title: Bucks looking to remain competitive as they discuss Giannis Antetokounmpo trade
item.description: League sources say the Bucks want to remain competitive next season. They don’t outright own any of their firsts until 2031, and even if they did, the flattened draft odds don’t incentivize teams to bottom out. And the best player on the table is, by far, Jaylen Brown.

item.title: Bucks and Celtics have haggled over what pieces would …
item.description: League sources say the Bucks and Celtics have haggled over what pieces would be attached to Brown: the 27th pick? How many future firsts? Hugo Gonzalez? Payton Pritchard? The Celtics have been reluctant to give up much more than Brown.

item.title: Tarris Reed was in for a predraft workout with the …
item.description: John Karalis: Draft News: A league source tells me UConn's Tarris Reed was in for a predraft workout with the Celtics this past weekend in a group that also included Arizona's Jaden Bradley, Illinois' Ben Humrichous, Miami (OH)'s Peter Suder, and UNC's Seth Trimble.

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo willing to sign extension with Celtics if traded
item.description: Adam Himmelsbach: Regarding Giannis Antetokounmpo…Celtics are in the mix. Giannis’ camp has indicated he’d sign extension with Boston. But it remains unclear how close the Cs and Bucks are to a potential deal.

item.title: Celtics not actively shopping Jaylen Brown?
item.description: Shams Charania: "Everyone around the league is going to be talking about this. If this deal does not get done for the Celtics, what is the future of Jaylen Brown? The thing the Celtics do have though, and Jaylen Brown knows this, is he's got multiple years left on his deal. To my knowledge, they're not shopping Jaylen Brown. This is a very specific situation. This is now twice in four years where the Celtics have offered Jaylen Brown in a major trade for a future Hall of Famer. In 2022, they off

item.title: Celtics not willing to add multiple firsts for Giannis Antetokounmpo
item.description: Heat Central: The Bucks owners are “split” on which decision to make with a Giannis trade and Boston is NOT willing to add multiple 1st round picks like Miami, per @JakeLFischer “We’re being told there is some type of split faction in MIL right now among the major stakeholders for the Bucks… the Celtics to our understanding, are not willing to offer the same level of multiple years of draft capital that the Miami Heat are” (Via @BleacherReport)

item.title: Blazers will be looking to trade for Anthony Davis is he becomes available
item.description: In his latest NBA insider report for Bleacher Report, Jake Fischer announced that the Blazers are open to the possibility of trading for the star big man should the Washington Wizards like to entertain a trade. "Portland, to our understanding, as they have been floating around and poking around in Giannis trade conversations, we've been told that they would look to pivot to Anthony Davis on the trade market," Fischer said. "They obviously have Jaylen Brown interest as well. "We've been told that

item.title: He (Giannis) is on his way back from the states as of …
item.description: Heat Central: Giannis is on his way back from the states and ready for a trade to Miami or Boston, per @ShamsCharania "At this very hour the MIL Bucks are moving closer and closer to a trade resolution for Giannis.... He (Giannis) is on his way back from the states as of right now. He's ready for a trade whether it's Miami or Boston"

item.title: Celtics want to keep Hugo Gonzalez
item.description: Bill Simmons: I’m just gonna say…. I would be absolutely shocked if Hugo was/is thrown into any Giannis trade offer. The entire organization is all-in on him - Stevens prob the most. They could not be higher on him. I genuinely don’t believe he’s involved. Guess we’ll see.

item.title: The Celtics, as Bill says, certainly prefer to keep …
item.description: Marc Stein: The Bucks, as we reported last night, have certainly asked for Hugo. The Celtics, as Bill says, certainly prefer to keep him. Not exactly a make-or-break element but one of the variables that factors into this Boston vs. Miami struggle.

item.title: Chris Haynes reporting that Celtics are the favorites …
item.description: Barry Jackson: Chris Haynes reporting that Celtics are the favorites to acquire Giannis tonight or tomorrow

item.title: The Celtics registered interest in Antetokounmpo at …
item.description: The Bucks seriously negotiated with two finalists in recent weeks: the Heat and Boston Celtics, who were both on Antetokounmpo's list of preferred destinations. The Celtics registered interest in Antetokounmpo at the trade deadline in February, and aggressively pursued him this time around, offering Milwaukee a package featuring 2024 Finals MVP Jaylen Brown and two first-round picks, according to sources.

item.title: Brad Stevens has not actively shopped Brown in trade …
item.description: Celtics president Brad Stevens has not actively shopped Brown in trade talks, sources said, but the franchise has now offered him twice for future Hall of Famers over the past four offseasons. In 2022, Brown was the centerpiece of Boston's proposal to the Brooklyn Nets for Kevin Durant.

item.title: Kasparas Jakucionis was Miami's version of Boston's …
item.description: Jake Fischer: Kasparas Jakucionis was Miami's version of Boston's Hugo Gonzalez hang-up. Had been hearing all day the Heat did not want to part with the Illinois product on top of their Herro-Ware-Jacquez-No. 13 structure. That's an All-Star guard, three recent first-round picks still on rookie deals, a second lottery pick in tomorrow's loaded draft class, plus two more future firsts and a swap... It's really a sizable haul for Giannis Antetokounmpo. Compared to Jaylen Brown and two first-round

item.title: Bucks didn't want to risk Jaylen Brown wanting out of Milwaukee?
item.description: Kevin O'Connor: Sources say Bucks owner Jimmy Haslem was a driving force in taking the Heat deal for Giannis Antetokounmpo over Boston’s offer. Haslem didn’t want to risk Jaylen Brown wanting out of Milwaukee in a year after dealing with Giannis and Myles Garrett trade demands. Haslem wanted certainty, and the Miami deal provided more of that with more total assets in return to Milwaukee.

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo was open to signing an extension with Heat, Celtics and Timberwolves before trade
item.description: Chris Haynes: Sources: The 3 teams Giannis Antetokounmpo was open to signing an extension with: Miami, Boston and Minnesota. The Heat land a foundational piece and now have the attention of top players searching for a new home.

item.title: Landing Giannis may force Heat to part ways with Norman Powell
item.description: Bobby Marks: I think it potentially could cost you Norman Powell, your All-Star from last year, who is an unrestricted free agent. Remember, if we deal with these apron rules, right? Miami now becomes hard-capped at the first apron. They're limited as far as what they can go out and offer Norman Powell and the deal. And when you look at their back court, their back court is a little bit depleted here. They need a little bit more front court help. And they've got about five roster spots to go out

item.title: I think the biggest thing is trying to repair …
item.description: Bobby Marks: Jaylen Brown still has three years left on his contract, making roughly $185 million. If Jaylen Brown was going into the last year of his contract, I think basically all leverage would have been lost here. Now, there's some relationship repairing that needs to happen. As Shams said earlier, yes, 2022 he was certainly talked about with Kevin Durant. Now, four years later with Giannis. I think the difference now is that Jaylen Brown is a top 10 player in the NBA and he was basically b

item.title: Especially given the timing, the Celtics’ decision …
item.description: Especially given the timing, the Celtics’ decision could bother Jaylen Brown. Since the last time he went through such trade rumors, he won the 2024 championship and the NBA Finals MVP award. Last season was his best — and not just because he set career highs with 28.7 points, 6.9 rebounds and 5.1 assists per game with the injured Jayson Tatum on the sideline. Teammates believed Brown’s leadership enabled them to exceed expectations. Other All-Stars might have expressed frustration about losing

item.title: Teams preparing for 'bidding war' for Jaylen Brown after Celtics miss on Giannis
item.description: Heat Central: Teams are now preparing to send offers for Jaylen Brown after Celtics strikes out on Giannis, Per @WindhorstESPN “What I expect to happen, is a bidding war for Jaylen Brown. In the most recent days, teams have been preparing for this eventuality… I think it’ll take time to play out, but if there is a draft pick involved, it could happen today” We got Giannis and Blew up Boston all in one

item.title: “The Celtics and Jaylen Brown will have hard …
item.description: Get Up: “The Celtics and Jaylen Brown will have hard conversations moving forward about the fallout from a trade that didn't happen. ... You've gotta get a deal done if you're the Celtics." — @ShamsCharania on what the Giannis trade to Miami means for Jaylen Brown

item.title: Jaylen Brown is accepting this and realizes this is a …
item.description: Brown enjoyed his more central role with the Celtics this past season while Jayson Tatum rehabbed his torn Achilles until March. Brown was a second team All-NBA selection and was even in the MVP conversations at various points. "From what I understand, Jaylen Brown is accepting this and realizes this is a chance to turn the page in his career and he may be getting his own team," said ESPN's Brian Windhorst on Monday's episode of First Take. "Which is something he's thought about for some time."

item.title: "If Luka comes up that's a compliment. If Giannis …
item.description: ‪Oh No He Didn't‬: Richard Jefferson on Jaylen Brown's name being mentioned in trade rumors: "If Luka comes up that's a compliment. If Giannis comes up that's a compliment, Jokic, if Shai, if those names come up you are a part of that class.

item.title: John Wall on Jaylen Brown: He wants his own team, he needs his own team
item.description: John Wall: "I feel like it is that time. I love him and Jayson Tatum together, they won a championship. You always get these conversations when you have two guys that think they are 1A. It is always difficult and when you let the media get involved or the outside noise get involved, it can mess up a team. I think when he won Finals MVP, it kind of shook a lot of people up. I think his comments from this year when he came out and was like, 'This is my favorite year of all.' Why would you say that

item.title: "The Celtics were not willing to part with enough …
item.description: NBA on ESPN: "The Celtics were not willing to part with enough young players and assets to get a deal done." @ShamsCharania says Boston offered Jaylen Brown and two first-round picks for Giannis Antetokounmpo before he was traded to Miami 👀

item.title: Bucks were intrigued by idea of making Jaylen Brown franchise centerpiece
item.description: According to league sources, the Bucks were very intrigued with the idea of making Brown their new franchise centerpiece but also had serious concerns about whether history might repeat itself if he came to town. Brown, the five-time All-Star who is coming off the best season of his career and finished sixth in MVP voting, has three seasons and a combined $183 million left on his current deal. But if he didn’t want to be in Milwaukee, as some signs suggested in recent weeks, then the Bucks might

item.title: Celtics listening to trade offers for Jaylen Brown
item.description: Pat McAffee: "The whole league is paying very close attention to the Jaylen Brown and Boston Celtics situation.. My understanding is that the Celtics are listening to interested teams and offers for Jaylen Brown" -- Shams Charania

item.title: Myles Turner and Kyle Kuzma trade candidates for Bucks?
item.description: With Giannis Antetokounmpo and Bobby Portis gone, all eyes turn to holdovers Myles Turner and Kyle Kuzma as trade candidates for the Bucks. Turner has always known that if Antetokounmpo was ever traded, he’d be a strong trade candidate, HoopsHype has learned. During his Pacers tenure, Turner was linked to the Hornets, Pelicans, and Lakers in trade rumors at various points. Those teams could all make sense now, and possibly the Celtics. Turner, 30, is still owed $83.55 million, including a player

item.title: “[The] Brooklyn [Nets] and the [Los Angeles] Clippers …
item.description: Sam Amick: “[The] Brooklyn [Nets] and the [Los Angeles] Clippers were mentioned as teams that were out front in that race, but I think the list is very long. I think that Detroit comes to mind. [With] Boston, actually, Trey is seen as a potential plan B for them if they miss out on Giannis, which they obviously did. So, Trey is going to have a lot of suitors, and I think that could be a draft-night deal. “There was even a scenario floating around with Trey going to the [Atlanta] Hawks in a deal

item.title: Myles Turner thought he would be traded to the Celtics and Lakers at different points in his career
item.description: Myles Turner on dealing with constant NBA trade rumors: "I'm so numb to it now. At the very beginning of my career, I was in a trade rumor every single offseason. I had my bags packed twice. I thought at one point in my career I was going to be traded to Boston for Gordon Hayward and I thought I literally was ready to go and then that fell through at the last second. And then I also thought I was going to be traded to the Lakers for a while. So I'm so numb to it now, if it happens it happens and

item.title: Celtics now listening to trade offers for Jaylen Brown
item.description: Shams Charania: "I am told the hard conversations between these two sides have started. This has been a long relationship between Jaylen Brown and the Celtics. It has been mostly fruitful when you think about the success that these two sides have had. But I am told here, as we are hours away from the NBA draft, the Boston Celtics have started to listen, engage and discuss trades around Jaylen Brown. Sources tell me the Celtics are speaking to teams right now about Jaylen Brown. In 2022, the Celt

item.title: “That doesn’t mean a trade will necessarily happen.”
item.description: Daniel Donabedian: Shams Charania repeats that the Celtics are engaged in trade talks about Jaylen Brown, but said nothing may come of it: “That doesn’t mean a trade will necessarily happen.”

item.title: League sources say Golden State and Atlanta are …
item.description: A subplot I'm watching tonight: Will Trey Murphy III finally get traded out of New Orleans? There has been all kinds of chatter about the 26-year-old small forward being on the move of late, with a laundry list of teams interested. League sources say Golden State and Atlanta are currently vetting scenarios in which they'd trade their pick (No. 8 and No. 11, respectively) to land him. He's been tied to Brooklyn (pre-Julius Randle trade), the Clippers (No. 5 pick) and Boston (No. 27) as well.

item.title: Derrick White has been named President of Basketball …
item.description: Noa Dalzell: Derrick White has been named President of Basketball Operations for the Colorado Men’s Basketball Team

item.title: Celtics will select Chris Cenac at No. 27
item.description: Jeff Goodman: Boston will select Houston freshman Chris Cenac Jr. with the No. 27 pick, source told @thefieldof68. The 6-11, 240-pound skilled forward averaged 9.5 points and 7.9 boards this past season for the Cougars.

item.title: "Just like any other time, I'm not gonna talk about …
item.description: Bobby Krivitsky: Brad Stevens when asked how close the Celtics were to trading Jaylen Brown for Giannis: "Just like any other time, I'm not gonna talk about those type of things."

item.title: Brad Stevens: 'Jaylen Brown is a big part of us'
item.description: Justin Turpin: “Jaylen Brown is a big part of us. I'm never going to predict the future, but every indication, everything that I think about over the past few years has been building around those guys. So obviously, you never know. "But at the same time, the one thing I want to make very clear is how valued he's always been. He's been amazing…. And whether that run ends 10 years from now when he retires, or before, there's a lot to celebrate. We have a great relationship, an open relationship wh

item.title: Celtics interested in Evan Mobley?
item.description: Chris Mannix: Teams are looking to see if Jaylen Brown is truly available and what the asking price for him is going to be. But the Celtics, I believe, are listening to those calls and are thinking up their own trade matches of their own. I spent some time this afternoon talking to different teams about what might the Celtics be interested in. And the name in the team that came back to me a lot was Evan Mobley in the Cleveland Cavaliers. Evan Mobley, young, big, just entering the prime of his ca

item.title: Danny Green: Celtics trade talk around Jaylen Brown is 'disrespectful'
item.description: Danny Green on Celtics trying to trade Jaylen Brown for Giannis: This is disrespectful. And I'm not even that type of person. But y'all could kick rocks, bro. I’m out of here. I'm gone. I'm cool. Y'all don't want me, I'm cool. Like, not only did it not fall through, but they're still talking about we're still entertaining idea. Like right now, they're still entertaining trades.

item.title: Brad Stevens was asked if he still believes Tatum and …
item.description: Justin Turpin: Brad Stevens was asked if he still believes Tatum and Brown can bring a championship: “Yes,” he said.

item.title: Celtics willing to move Jaylen Brown for a center
item.description: Boston continued to discuss Brown trades on Tuesday, two sources familiar with the talks tell Sports Illustrated. Many of the conversations were initiated by rival teams, sources say. Some were brokered by the Celtics. “They are hunting a big man,” says a rival executive. “And they are willing to trade [Brown] to get it.”

item.title: Brown was upset when his name came up in trade talks …
item.description: Brown was upset when his name came up in trade talks in 2022, sources said back then. It would be unsurprising if he was seething now. Stevens made the point that he has stayed in regular contact with Brown’s representatives the last few days—“We certainly wanted to be as proactive and upfront with that as possible,” Stevens said—while reiterating his value to the team.

item.title: The urgency from either side could change as the weeks …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Shams: "The Boston Celtics are actively engaged in trade talks surrounding Jaylen Brown with multiple interested teams. The urgency from either side could change as the weeks go on. His status is very much up in the air in Boston"

item.title: Windhorst: There is a very good chance Jaylen Brown is playing elsewhere to start next season
item.description: Bobby Krivitsky: Brian Windhorst: “I would believe that there’s a very good chance Jaylen Brown is gonna be elsewhere to start next season.” : ESPN

item.title: Celtics select Dillon Mitchell at No. 40
item.description: Jeff Goodman: Boston selects Dillon Mitchell at No. 40, source told @TheFieldOf68

item.title: Boston Celtics will select Dillon Mitchell with the …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Boston Celtics will select Dillon Mitchell with the No. 40 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft.

item.title: Celtics offers Bucks Jaylen Brown, two first rounders for Giannis Antetokounmpo
item.description: Shams Charania: The Celtics aggressively pursued Giannis, offering Milwaukee a package featuring 2024 Finals MVP Jaylen Brown and two first-round picks, sources tell ESPN. The Bucks seriously negotiated with two finalists in recent weeks: Miami and Boston, which were both on Antetokounmpo’s list of preferred destinations.

item.title: Jaylen Brown: 'ESPN is unethical, and Stephen A. Smith is the head face of that'
item.description: Jaylen Brown: "ESPN is unethical, and Stephen A. Smith is the head face of that. The organization, the players, they were all in agreeaance. They all knew what I meant by that. Our team was basically salary dumped. We lost a lot of players. For us to emerge and still be in the same position..."

item.title: Mike Zarren on how the NBA possibly expanding to add 2 …
item.description: Bobby Krivitsky: Mike Zarren on how the NBA possibly expanding to add 2 new teams impacts the Celtics' current planning: "We don't know if the rules for an expansion draft will be the same as they were last time...The more good contracts you have, the more good contracts you'll have left after an expansion draft... The more good players you can have...the more that will be left." He also notes expansion isn't guaranteed.

item.title: Boston has apparently decided that Jaylen Brown …
item.description: Brian Windhorst: "The most important thing in the league right now for what is going to happen in the 2026-27 season is that Boston has apparently decided that Jaylen Brown probably needs to be traded for them to be competitive. Because that is now going to be the next storyline in the NBA. Obviously, the Heat getting Giannis is a huge story for them. From the moment they traded Jimmy Butler, they have been on the hunt for the next star. That is a huge story for them."

item.title: Lowe: 'I don't think Jaylen Brown starts the season with the Boston Celtics next year'
item.description: CelticsBlog: Zach Lowe on his podcast: "It feels like the the toothpaste is too far out of the tube with Jaylen Brown." "If you ask me right now... I don't think Jaylen Brown starts the season with the Boston Celtics next year. That could end up being wrong. I'm not saying that definitively, I'm saying if you made me bet, yes or no, is Jaylen Brown a Celtic next year, I'm betting no"

item.title: Charania: Celtics actively in trade discussions involving Jaylen Brown with multiple teams
item.description: Shams Charania: "Jaylen Brown was offered for Giannis Antetokounmpo, and so that is not the end for him in trade rumors. The Boston Celtics are actively in conversations with interested teams in Jaylen Brown. The sense that I get around the league is that the Celtics are having these discussions with a level of purpose. It does not mean that a trade is guaranteed because Jaylen Brown has three years guaranteed left on his deal on a supermax contract. Finding the right suitor that meets the right

item.title: Chris Mannix on the impact of trade rumors on Jaylen …
item.description: Chris Mannix on the impact of trade rumors on Jaylen Brown's relationship with the Celtics: "I do think it's more damaged than you think. All the Jaylen Brown trade rumors in Boston. It was Kawhi Leonard in 2018. It was Anthony Davis in 19 and 20. It was Kevin Durant in 2022. But this to me feels different. In the early years, Jaylen Brown was just like this young kid that had proven nothing. In 2022, he had proven something, and I reported at the time that he was pretty pissed off at those trad

item.title: Among the young names included are Izan Almansa, Mario …
item.description: The Spanish national team will face Denmark on July 2 in Madrid before traveling to Tbilisi to take on Georgia on July 5. Spain enters the final window with a perfect 4-0 record and has already secured a place in the next qualification round. Veteran center Willy Hernangomez will captain a roster that combines experienced internationals with some of the country's most promising young players. Among the young names included are Izan Almansa, Mario Saint-Supery, Alvaro Cardenas, and Boston Celtics

item.title: Chris Cenac on Joe Mazzulla: 'Definitely going to be a Hall of Fame coach in the future'
item.description: Daniel Donabedian: Celtics rookie Chris Cenac Jr. had this to say about Joe Mazzulla after getting drafted last night: "Great coach. Definitely going to be a Hall of Fame coach in the future. So I'm looking forward to playing with him, for him."

item.title: I don't know if you can actually go out on the market …
item.description: Marcus Morris on Jaylen Brown: "We are talking about one of the guys that carried this team, that has been the longest tenured Celtic on the team. I had the privilege to play with him and Jason Tatum and watch these guys grow up. This is a mature young fellow in this league, he is in his prime right now. I don't know if you can actually go out on the market and find a better player that plays defense and offense at the highest level, being a great two-way player. He is a leader, he is a motivato

item.title: Celtics to sign Milos Uzan
item.description: Jake: The Boston Celtics and Houston’s Milos Uzan have agreed on a deal, source told @Stockrisers.

item.title: Timberwolves had discussions with Celtics on potential Jaylen Brown trade before LaMelo Ball deal
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: The Wolves did have discussions with the Celtics on Jaylen Brown as well, sources told The Athletic. But they saw LaMelo Ball as fitting more of what they needed, for likely less compensation going out.

item.title: @Tucker_DeVries has signed an Exhibit-10 contract with …
item.description: Indiana Basketball: Boston bound. ☘️ @Tucker_DeVries has signed an Exhibit-10 contract with the @celtics !

item.title: Hornets in the mix for Jaylen Brown?
item.description: Mike Lacett: I’m hearing the @hornets are one of these teams

item.title: “He’s going to get traded,” the analyst said on ESPN’s …
item.description: According to Brian Windhorst, Boston is actively discussing Brown with multiple teams and expects those conversations to continue as the organization considers reshaping its roster. “He’s going to get traded,” the analyst said on ESPN’s Unsportsmanlike. Windhorst also noted that while Celtics president Brad Stevens praised Brown during his recent media availability, he never ruled out the possibility of a trade. “He obviously told him that there was a good chance they’re going to trade him,” Win

item.title: I think that the Celtics were asking more for Brown …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: "They were in on Jaylen Brown as well. It was made clear by the Celtics that he was going to be available and the Wolves were trying to have conversations about what it would take to get Jaylen Brown. I think that they looked at those two, Jaylen Brown and LaMelo Ball, and Brown, just because he's won a championship, he's a little older, he was top-six MVP this year, I think that the Celtics were asking more for Brown than what the Hornets wanted for Ball."

item.title: The Raptors were another rumored suitor for the point …
item.description: Ball, 24, is under contract for the next three seasons and is set to make $40.8 million next year as a running mate for Anthony Edwards in Minnesota. The Raptors were another rumored suitor for the point guard and league sources confirmed to MassLive that the Celtics did not pursue a deal for the former Hornets guard.

item.title: Celtics seeking at least four first-round picks for Jaylen Brown
item.description: Shams Charania on Jaylen Brown: My understanding is in some cases the Celtics have asked teams for at least four first-round picks. The part that makes it tricky here is Jaylen Brown's got three years left on his deal at a super max clip. Which team meets that valuation so far that the Celtics have set in some regard with four first-round picks? And I think throughout all of this, the question is going to be which side will influence the urgency of a trade? Because if he's got three years left,

item.title: Nikola Vucevic: I want to keep playing in the NBA
item.description: Nikola Vucevic: ‘My focus is truly on the NBA; my desire is to stay there. As I’ve said before, it is the best league. I’ve been there for 15 years and I would like to stay there until the end; I believe I still have a place there and the quality to play at a high level. Regarding Red Star (Zvezda), I’ve said before that if I ever decide to return to Europe, Zvezda would be one of the favorites—everyone knows that. But for now, that really isn't the focus. I don't want to create unrealistic desi

item.title: Nikola Vucevic says adjusting to Celtics bench role was 'the hardest thing'
item.description: Nikola Vucevic on Celtics: ‘The hardest thing for me was that I had been a starter for such a long number of years and seasons, where I had my set minutes, thirty-plus minutes, a certain number of shots, and a defined role. When you play like that, you know what is expected of you and you are used to your own rhythm. Coming to Boston, I was prepared for the possibility that I would very likely be coming off the bench if a trade occurred. Regarding me personally, it wasn’t a problem to adapt from

item.title: Nikola Vucevic blasts Europeans for calling the NBA 'just a show'
item.description: Nikola Vucevic: 'What irritates me is that people from Europe say the NBA is just a show. If people had watched Boston or Oklahoma City or San Antonio all year, they would realize that NBA teams play defense. It looks different because the players are so good and so powerful and talented that everything they do looks so easy and beautiful. It’s like watching a Formula 1 driver; they take corners and brake without a problem, but if you tried to do it, it wouldn't go so easily. In the NBA, they le

item.title: Celtics still engaging in trade discussions for Jaylen Brown
item.description: Ultimately, though the Celtics were willing to part with Brown, they weren't willing to also include young prospects Hugo Gonzalez and Baylor Scheierman to land Antetokounmpo, a decision that has put them in an awkward situation with Brown. Multiple sources say the team is still engaging in trade discussions for the five-time All-Star. Says one league source: "It'd be insane for Boston to bring him back after dangling him so publicly." Says another: "I wouldn't do a damn thing. Those two guys ar

item.title: In Miami and Boston, however, sleep was hard to come …
item.description: HORST SLEPT WELL Sunday night and longer than he usually does, relieved to be on the other side of the most consequential decision of his professional life. In Miami and Boston, however, sleep was hard to come by. Both franchises believed they had a great chance of landing Antetokounmpo, according to team sources, but they were paralyzed until a final decision had been made.

item.title: Sparking a series of organizational discussions that …
item.description: The Celtics had lost in the first round to the Philadelphia 76ers, sparking a series of organizational discussions that led to the conclusion that the current roster simply "wasn't good enough," as one Celtics source put it.

item.title: Celtics wanted Rudy Gobert, Naz Reid, first-round pick and pick swaps from Timberwolves for Jaylen Brown
item.description: The Timberwolves explored multiple avenues to add another star next to Edwards in the days leading up to landing Ball. There were discussions with the Chicago Bulls about Josh Giddey. They had talks with the Boston Celtics about Jaylen Brown, a team source said, but the Celtics were looking for a package including Gobert, Reid and similar pick compensation that went to Charlotte for Ball, team sources said. That was just too much for the Wolves to pay for a soon-to-be 30-year-old forward who, wh

item.title: Per source, Amile Jefferson will be head coach of the …
item.description: Adam Himmelsbach: Per source, Amile Jefferson will be head coach of the Celtics’ summer league team. Have to assume Jayson Tatum will make an appearance in Vegas to support his guy.

item.title: Pistons exploring sign-and-trade option of Jalen Duren for Jaylen Brown
item.description: Jake Fischer: There’s also a potential backstop emerging for Boston’s Jaylen Brown trade discussions. Detroit is very much weighing the prospect of signing-and-trading restricted free agent Jalen Duren for Brown, sources say, among other conceptual outcomes, if that option remains available for the Pistons once free agency opens on Tuesday early evening. We have it all @TheSteinLine: http://tinyurl.com/4d92xes6

item.title: “I really am skeptical that you’re gonna see Jaylen …
item.description: John-Griner Canady: .@JakeLFischer adds more context to the Celtics asking price of four first-round picks for Jaylen Brown “I really am skeptical that you’re gonna see Jaylen Brown moved for just draft capital, because the word prevails from New England that the Celtics want to be a contender.”

item.title: Blazers actively pursuing Jaylen Brown
item.description: There is one team leaguewide, sources say, most frequently described as actively pursuing Boston's Jaylen Brown. The Portland Trail Blazers. The Blazers have been known to covet Brown dating back to Damian Lillard's first tenure with the franchise. And they still do.

item.title: Had, not an executive, but an analytics guy, saying …
item.description: SiriusXM NBA Radio: 'There’s mixed feelings about him.' 🤯 'The analytics on Jaylen Brown are not good. I'm more of an eye-test guy. There are some people out there that look at that a little more deeper than the eye test says. I had, not an executive, but an analytics guy, saying to me ‘we view Jaylen Brown as the seventh-best player on the team.'😳 @BobbyMarks42 tells @BGeltzNBA and @SamMitchellNBA the market for Jaylen Brown isn’t as robust as it might seem. Listen to the full show: https://sxm

item.title: Celtics to re-sign Ron Harper Jr. on a three-year, $9 million deal
item.description: Shams Charania: Boston Celtics G/F Ron Harper Jr. intends to sign a new three-year, $9 million deal to return to the franchise, sources tell ESPN. The Celtics are declining Harper's $2.6M team option, clearing the way for the sides to commit on a longer deal.

item.title: Eddie A Johnson: Not surprising coming from a …
item.description: NBA Central: Jaylen Brown’s trade value isn’t that high around the league, per @BobbyMarks42 “I had an analytics guy tell me, ‘We view [Jaylen Brown] as the seventh-best player on a TEAM.’”😳 Eddie A Johnson: Not surprising coming from a mathematical cubicle nerd!

item.title: Sam Quinn: There's a strain of NBA thought that …
item.description: NBA Central: Jaylen Brown’s trade value isn’t that high around the league, per @BobbyMarks42 “I had an analytics guy tell me, ‘We view [Jaylen Brown] as the seventh-best player on a TEAM.’”😳 Sam Quinn: There's a strain of NBA thought that suggests that basically the only players with trade value are guys on rookie contracts, MVPs in their 20s and 3-and-D wings on below-market contract. It goes too far.

item.title: Stephen A Smith: Now this is utterly ridiculous. I …
item.description: NBACentral: Jaylen Brown’s trade value isn’t that high around the league, per @BobbyMarks42 “I had an analytics guy tell me, ‘We view [Jaylen Brown] as the seventh-best player on a TEAM.’” Stephen A Smith: Now this is utterly ridiculous. I have no doubt that @BobbyMarks42 was told what he said he was told (He’s incredible at what he does) — but there is no way in hell @FCHWPO is the 7th-best player on any team in the NBA. He may be the best 2-way player in the league. Yes, it’s me saying this. F

item.title: Nuggets to join Jaylen Brown's sweepstakes?
item.description: Sources say that the Nuggets have at least internally discussed whether they have the asset wherewithal to join the trade chase for Boston's Jaylen Brown, but the Nuggets have yet to emerge as a bonafide landing spot for the Celtics' MVP candidate.

item.title: Kyle Mangas, who was briefly on a two-way contract …
item.description: Adam Himmelsbach: Per source, veteran guard Kyle Mangas, who was briefly on a two-way contract with the Spurs last season, is expected to be added to the Celtics’ summer league roster.

item.title: Bobby Marks explained that Jaylen Brown’s trade value …
item.description: Bobby Marks explained that Jaylen Brown’s trade value has gone down and reported what an analyst told him. He said, “I had one, not an executive, but an analytics guy saying, ‘Yeah, we view them as like the seventh best player on a team.”

item.title: Brown started crashing out after reading Marks’ …
item.description: Brown started crashing out after reading Marks’ comment. He said, “Analytics nowadays used to discredit and control narratives – Roll the ball out none of these guys better than me on both ends who does he work for.” Marks then pointed out that he would want the Celtics star on his team, and that he doesn’t prefer analytics. He also added that Brown would surely help any team in the league win a championship, citing his impactful winning record in the last four years ((191-80 in the last 4 years

item.title: Brown then posted on X, “Nobody has won more combined …
item.description: Brown then posted on X, “Nobody has won more combined regular-season and playoff games since I entered the league 10 years ago,” before claiming that analytics are ‘ruining’ hoops right now. He also asked Marks to state his source and identify the analyst who made the comment.

item.title: Deni Avdija off the table in trade talks?
item.description: The question for Boston with the Blazers will be what type of package they would demand from Portland. The Blazers have plenty of intriguing young talent across their roster but they would certainly want to retain the majority of it to compete in the Western Conference even after a Brown acquisition. All-Star Deni Avdija would not be on the table in any trade talks, league sources tell MassLive and it would be tough for Boston to pry away Donovan Clingan as well. The Blazers do not have strong c

item.title: Jaylen Brown shared this video on his Instagram that …
item.description: Fullcourtpass: Jaylen Brown shared this video on his Instagram that talks about… - How he was “the best player” on a championship team - How he got “blackballed” by the Olympic team - How the Celtics tried to trade him multiple times (h/t @Savageboston)

item.title: “Listen, I’m more of an eye test guy,” he said. “I …
item.description: “The original clip that was posted this morning did not have the full context of Bobby’s exact answer,” Geltzeiler wrote. “And in this case, it’s VERY important to hear everything he said.” The fuller quotes provide a bit more context around what Marks was saying and how he disagreed with the analytics take on Brown. “Listen, I’m more of an eye test guy,” he said. “I like impact winning, 29 years old, has played in 60 more games, 4 to 5 last year. They’re 191-80 the last 4 years he plays. Okay?

item.title: Marks also tried to assuage Brown, responding to his …
item.description: Marks also tried to assuage Brown, responding to his post to say he didn’t agree with the analytics guys’ assessment. “But what wasn’t posted was the 2m of saying I’m not an analytics person but an eye test guy,” Marks wrote to Brown. “That I would want Jaylen Brown on my team. That you impact winning (191-80 in the last 4 years), is a top-10 player and would help any team looking to win a championship. That part of course didn’t make it. But that’s the media in 2026.”

item.title: Several teams are expected to show interest in signing …
item.description: Several teams are expected to show interest in signing Williams as an unrestricted free agent, including the Phoenix Suns, Golden State Warriors, Boston Celtics, and the Mavericks, who want to retain him, league sources told HoopsHype. Dallas has Early Bird Rights on Williams entering free agency this summer.

item.title: Deni Avdija was not on the table in trade talks for …
item.description: Trail Blazers All-Star forward Deni Avdija was not on the table in trade talks for Antetokounmpo, and he remains off the table in Brown discussions as of now, league sources told HoopsHype. Portland’s other young players, such as Scoot Henderson and Shaedon Sharpe, are considered available. With Boston considering center upgrades and needing to replace Brown’s position if he’s traded, Portland’s Donovan Clingan and 2025 All-Defensive forward Toumani Camara would both be coveted by Boston. Howeve

item.title: Aaron Gordon has drawn trade interest from Blazers; Celtics have kept tabs
item.description: Nuggets forward Aaron Gordon was considered among the contingency plans for the Heat if the franchise didn’t land Giannis Antetokounmpo via trade, league sources told HoopsHype. Similarly, the Portland Trail Blazers have expressed trade interest in Gordon should they not land Jaylen Brown, their preferred target, HoopsHype has learned. Ironically, the Celtics have also kept tabs on Gordon from afar.

item.title: Cam Johnson has drawn trade interest from Lakers, Celtics, Heat, Clippers, Magic
item.description: Meanwhile, as the Nuggets navigate getting below the second apron, sharpshooting forward Cam Johnson has drawn trade interest from several teams, including the Los Angeles Lakers, Boston Celtics, Miami Heat, Los Angeles Clippers, and Orlando Magic, league sources told HoopsHype. The 30-year-old forward shot career-highs from the field (.480) and beyond the arc (.430), and is on an expiring $23.06 million contract for the upcoming season.

item.title: Cedric Maxwell on beef between Jaylen Brown, Stephen A. Smith: 'Why is an elephant right now battling with an ant?'
item.description: Cedric Maxwell: "But the other side of that, they gave him $300 million. If you're that mad with me and you give me $300 million, you can dangle me around like a carrot. I could care less, but in this case, I do understand that players have egos and their egos could be bruised. We've seen him go back and forth with Stephen A. Smith over and over again. I always say why is an elephant right now battling with an ant? Jaylen Brown should be beyond that."

item.title: Cedric Maxwell: "I am a huge Jaylen Brown fan. I would …
item.description: Cedric Maxwell: "I am a huge Jaylen Brown fan. I would hate to see you lose him, but I understand that there's some business aspects that come into play. If I was the Celtics right now, I'd want a hell of a package for him because Jaylen Brown was one of the top 10 players, one of the top seven players in the NBA last year. He became All-NBA, All-World essentially, and one thing he said, 'I am right now the best two-way player in the NBA other than that the alien down in San Antonio.' He has a g

item.title: Cedric Maxwell: If I was the Celtics right now, I'd want a hell of a package for Jaylen Brown
item.description: Cedric Maxwell: I’m a huge Jaylen Brown fan. Let's start there. I would that you would lose him, but I understand that there's some business aspects that come into play. If I was the Celtics right now, I'd want a hell of a package for him because Jaylen Brown was one of the top 10 players, one of the top seven players in the NBA last year. He became All-NBA. And one thing he said, I am right now the best two-way player in the NBA other than, that the alien down in San Antonio.

item.title: Simmons: Jaylen Brown acting like he's done with Celtics
item.description: According to The Ringer’s Bill Simmons, folks around him during the French festival noted that Brown appears to have mentally moved on from Boston already. “The word on the street on Jaylen in France, and I know multiple people who were either in his vicinity or whatever, is that he was acting like he’s done in Boston, and that this is it, and I don’t think he’s very happy with this,” Simmons said during Sunday’s episode of his podcast.

item.title: No player in the NBA lowers the points that his …
item.description: Jeremias Engelmann: No player in the NBA lowers the points that his teammates score, while on the court with him, more than Jaylen Brown He lops off 4 points, per 100, from each of the 4 teammates that share the court with him

item.title: In contrast, a jump in Jaylen Brown's scoring output …
item.description: David Lee: stars tend to suppress their teammates' PPG, but they pair that scoring usage with hyper-efficiency (Giannis/prime Joel), influence high quality shots (Wemby), and/or suppress TOs (Luka). in contrast, a jump in Jaylen Brown's scoring output leads to diminishing returns for BOS👇

item.title: Nobody has won more combined regular season and …
item.description: Jaylen Brown: Nobody has won more combined regular season and playoff games since I entered the league 10 years ago

item.title: Analytics have / are ruining the game we playing AI …
item.description: Jaylen Brown: Analytics have / are ruining the game we playing AI hoops

item.title: Celtics were already shopping Jaylen Brown before Giannis talks heated up?
item.description: Brown hasn't said how he feels about the Celtics openly shopping him in trade scenarios, which sources have said took place with other teams even before the talks for Antetokounmpo heated up. The Celtics have three choices: (1) trade Brown to another team, which so far hasn't yielded strong offers; (2) extend Brown, hoping the financial commitment will assuage any hard feelings; or (3) bring him back without an extension and try to make him comfortable continuing on in this state of limbo.

item.title: If an analytic dude said that and he told you, why …
item.description: Paul Pierce: Paul Pierce on the comments saying Jaylen Brown analytics wise he’s the 7th best player on the Celtics: “I mean this is getting ridiculous. It’s gotten to the point where I’m no longer going to start watching any of the networks about sports. Like seriously this is just come on. I just feel like it’s clickbait. It doesn’t make sense. Like don’t even make that public. If an analytic dude said that and he told you, why would you make that public? Because it sounds silly. Jaylen Brown

item.title: There's a small pocket of execs and agents who believe …
item.description: There's a small pocket of execs and agents who believe the Celtics are bluffing with Brown, but the prevailing thought is they want to move on this -- soon.

item.title: Sam Amick: Celtics are asking for the sun and the moon in Jaylen Brown trade talks
item.description: Sam Amick on Jaylen Brown: "This one is, if I had to handicap it, it just feels like somebody has got to put enough on the table to get this thing done, but Boston is asking for the sun and the moon. I keep looking at Portland and because people keep bringing Portland up when I talk to them, they wanted to go big on Giannis Antetokounmpo and that did not work. Even without a commitment, they were willing to try to do a Giannis trade. And when you see that kind of boldness, then you kind of go, a

item.title: According to a league source, the Celtics are …
item.description: Adam Himmelsbach: According to a league source, the Celtics are exercising the team options of Neemias Queta, Jordan Walsh and Dalano Banton. Banton remains on a non-guaranteed deal. Per source, the Celtics are declining options of Amari Williams & Max Shulga, but they could still have opps w/Cs.

item.title: Jaylen Brown’s market has been described as “tepid” …
item.description: APHoops: Jaylen Brown’s market has been described as “tepid” with teams around the league not valuing Brown highly due to his contract and impending 2-year max contract extension, per @JakeLFischer.

item.title: Amari Williams will stay in Boston
item.description: Michael Scotto: JUST IN: Boston Celtics center Amari Williams has agreed to a deal to return to the Celtics, league sources told @hoopshype . Williams appeared in 22 games for Boston last season. He’s represented by agent George S. Langberg of @GSLSportsGroup .

item.title: I'm told this is a two-way deal for Williams
item.description: John Karalis: I'm told this is a two-way deal for Williams

item.title: Shams: Blazers not involved in Jaylen Brown talks, view acquiring Ja Morant as big move
item.description: SM Highlights: The Trail Blazers Aren’t Involved In Any Jaylen Brown Discussions. Per, @ShamsCharania “This was their big move as of right now my understanding is that they are not involved with Jaylen Brown, and they made attempts for Giannis, didn’t fall out.”

item.title: "If the price lowers at some point this offseason to 2 …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Shams on Jaylen Brown: "If the price lowers at some point this offseason to 2 firsts (round picks) or maybe 2 firsts and then some, then you might see something. My indication right now is they would not want to go that route"

item.title: Jaylen Brown has grown frustrated with Celtics approach after guiding team to 56 wins
item.description: League sources said that the Celtics have continued to field calls from teams interested in trading for Brown. A league source said that Brown has grown frustrated with the organization’s approach following a season in which he guided the Celtics to 56 wins while Jayson Tatum was sidelined for months with an Achilles injury.

item.title: One league source said Brown could still return to the …
item.description: It remains unclear if the Celtics are urgently attempting to trade Brown or simply listening to pitches from prospective suitors. One league source said Brown could still return to the Celtics next season, even if some uncomfortable conversations must happen first.

item.title: Robert Parish: 'Don’t get rid of a talent like Jaylen Brown unless he asked to be moved'
item.description: Parish is firmly against the idea of trading Brown, as the four-time champion adamantly expressed during an appearance Monday on SiriusXM NBA Radio. In explaining his position on the matter, Parish called out the Celtics, and specifically team president of basketball operations Brad Stevens, for what he deems a “serious miscalculation” by the organization following their pursuit of Giannis Antetokounmpo. “First of all, you don’t get rid of a talent like Jaylen Brown unless he asked to be moved,

item.title: Jaylen Brown has one of the best shooting touches in …
item.description: Jeremias Engelmann: Jaylen Brown has one of the best shooting touches in the NBA (as measured by actual points vs. expected points, based on shot location and type) Problem is, his shot selection is among the worst, trailing only DeMar DeRozan

item.title: You combine the contract that he's on, he's got three …
item.description: Shams Charania: "The conversation hasn't been about Jaylen Brown's game. Everyone knows Jaylen Brown is an elite player, but you combine the contract that he's on, he's got three years left on his supermax clip and where we're at in the offseason right now, what you're seeing is a price variance based on the player he is and the contract he's at and the assets that are available. There's a stark variance among those three categories."

item.title: If I'm Jaylen Brown, I feel some type of way coming …
item.description: Kendrick Perkins: "They could come out with all the reports that they want to, but again, some of the stuff, and I'm not saying he's a criminal, but I'm saying the streaming and stuff like that, that has been a turnoff to a lot of people. But if you're Brad Stevens, you cannot bring that energy back into your locker room because if I'm Jaylen Brown, I feel some type of way coming back and I'm saying, 'Well damn, you didn't try to trade me twice. How you expect me to act?' I just came off the bes

item.title: Deron Williams on Jaylen Brown: "Everything that he's …
item.description: Deron Williams on Jaylen Brown: "Everything that he's doing right now is very calculated. I guarantee you it's very calculated. I think he wants out."

item.title: According to ESPN’s Vincent Goodwill, one league …
item.description: Leonard’s future in Los Angeles is now growing murkier, the Clippers have reportedly emerged as a serious team to watch for Boston Celtics star Jaylen Brown. According to ESPN’s Vincent Goodwill, one league executive said the Clippers “want Brown bad,” adding another major name to what has already become one of the NBA’s most active rumor cycles.

item.title: Multiple executives still believe Portland could be …
item.description: Multiple executives still believe Portland could be involved in trade discussions for the Celtics' Jaylen Brown, and that could be the next big move. Has Morant hit rock bottom? One high-level exec told ESPN over the weekend that he believes Morant’s needs extend beyond basketball. He actually believes, however, that Morant can make a comeback to his old form because of how the guard competes when he’s healthy.

item.title: "This was their big move, Charania said. "As of right …
item.description: Here's what ESPN's Shams Charania had to say on "SportsCenter" when asked whether the Portland Trail Blazers could make a move for the Boston Celtics' Jaylen Brown after they traded for Ja Morant on Monday: "This was their big move, Charania said. "As of right now, my understanding is they're not involved with Jaylen Brown."

item.title: Clippers not involved in Jaylen Brown trade talks?
item.description: Also important: Sources say that the Clippers, whether or not their talks with the Raptors progress all the way to a completed deal, have not pursued Boston's Jaylen Brown this offseason. The sources added that the Clippers aren't planning to make a run at the Celtics' MVP candidate.

item.title: “I find [the Celtics shopping Jaylen Brown] …
item.description: SiriusXM NBA Radio: “I find [the Celtics shopping Jaylen Brown] disturbing” Celtics legend @RobertParish00 tells @AndrewBogusch and @joevardon why he is adamant about Boston keeping Brown and both sides repairing their relationship Hear our ongoing free agency coverage 👇 SXM.app.link/nbaradiolive

item.title: Listen, maybe they don’t want Jaylen Brown for other …
item.description: Draymond Green: Listen, maybe they don’t want Jaylen Brown for other reasons. But to say to be competitive, you need to trade Jaylen Brown. I don’t ride with that because I just watched them be competitive and be top two, three seed in the East with Jaylen Brown at the helm and Jayson Tatum pretty much out the entire season. So to say that we need to be competitive and you just won a championship a couple years ago, I don’t buy that. I don’t but in order to be competitive.

item.title: Celtics actively making teams trade offers involving Jaylen Brown
item.description: Brian Windhorst on Jaylen Brown: When the pursuit of Giannis Antetokounmpo last week failed, the expectation was that the Celtics would have a bespoke trade conversation with Jaylen Brown, targeting a couple of teams where they like the possible trade. And it's not gone that way. The Celtics have ended up canvasing a large part of the league. In some cases, from what I am told, actually actively making offers involving Brown, not receiving offers. When you have a valuable asset, you usually sit

item.title: So, right now, I do think the Celtics are searching a …
item.description: Brian Wimdhorst on Jaylen Brown: So, right now, I do think the Celtics are searching a little bit, and that has left some uncertainty. It has even raised the possibility that they would retreat totally and try to repair the relationship with Jaylen Brown. There's one way to do it, which would be to give him a contract extension next month and a huge amount of money. That said, I do not think that situation is over. I still think they're looking, and I still think he could be traded.

item.title: Celtics unlikely to trade Jaylen Brown under current asking price
item.description: A quick word on Jaylen Brown, as his sticky situation continues to loom so large over this offseason. Barring a drastic and unforeseen change in the market, the Boston Celtics will have to significantly lower their asking price if they plan on trading him anytime soon.

item.title: League sources have been tying the Blazers to Brown …
item.description: League sources have been tying the Blazers to Brown for some time now, but all indications are that their interest is moderate, at best. And, again, nothing close to what the Celtics have been asking for, according to sources who spoke on condition of anonymity to describe ongoing deliberations.

item.title: While the Nuggets have certainly considered the Brown …
item.description: While the Nuggets have certainly considered the Brown option, they haven’t spoken to the Celtics since the weekend and their interest has been significantly overstated, according to team sources who spoke on condition of anonymity to describe ongoing negotiations. There’s a familiar obstacle, too, as the Nuggets simply don’t have the draft assets to satisfy the Celtics’ ask.

item.title: Celtics planning to build a new arena in Boston?
item.description: The C’s have another decade on their lease at TD Garden, so Bill Chisholm has some time to decide. But not THAT much, given how long these projects typically take. And whispers are already out there. “They’ve been looking for a long time in Boston,” one political insider said. “Financially, it’s not working for them not to have their own stadium.” The latest rumored location? Outside South Station, on land currently occupied by the US Postal Service annex along Fort Point Channel.

item.title: Other sites beyond South Station are in the air as …
item.description: Other sites beyond South Station are in the air as well. One is the former Boston Flower Exchange in the South End, where The Abbey Group has approvals to develop four buildings. That property, however, is about a mile from four T stations, which might be tough on game days.

item.title: "Boston, I've heard all along, has this kind of …
item.description: Bobby Krivitsky: Jake Fischer on the Celtics and Jaylen Brown: "Boston, I've heard all along, has this kind of feeling in the back of their minds that they were already here in 2022." That's a reference to when the Celtics offered Brown in a deal for Kevin Durant before moving forward together. Fischer says what he initially heard was that Boston would only offer Brown for Giannis, but "the toothpaste is out of the tube." 🎥: NBA Free Agency Live, with Marc Stein and Jake Fischer on Bleacher Repo

item.title: Jalen Duren interested in Boston
item.description: Tomek Kordylewski: Here's @JakeLFischer during the B/R free agency LIVE: "I know Jalen Duren's side has some interest in the potential sign-and-trade exit not to L.A., not to Sacramento, but to Boston."

item.title: Jaylen Brown is out there,”
item.description: ESPN Cleveland: “James Harden opted out yesterday. He from what I understand is willing to partner with the Cavs to try and figure out how to improve their roster. Jaylen Brown is out there,” - @WindhorstESPN on the Cavs

item.title: Joe Mazzulla visited Hugo Gonzalez at the Spanish …
item.description: BasketNews: Joe Mazzulla visited Hugo Gonzalez at the Spanish national team training camp ☘️🇪🇸 #FIBAWC #StepItUp

item.title: Celtics pick up veteran point guard Mike Conley Jr.
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent guard Mike Conley Jr. has agreed to a one-year deal to sign with the Boston Celtics, sources tell ESPN. Conley becomes only the 14th player in NBA history to reach 20 seasons.

item.title: Mike Conley made the decision to head to Boston in the …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: Mike Conley made the decision to head to Boston in the last day or so, sources told @TheAthletic . An essential member of the Timberwolves, on and off the court, for the last 3 1/2 years.

item.title: Celtics 'strongly shopping' Jaylen Brown, taking offers from up to 10 teams so far
item.description: Shams Charania: Sources tell me the Celtics have actually started strongly shopping Jaylen Brown, soliciting offers from a market of about eight-to-10 teams in the last several days. It's unclear whether a deal is close or whether they necessarily stand, but there has been a high price threshold that the Celtics have set from the outset of any type of a Brown trade. .... They have asked some teams for at least four first-round picks. Just to be clear, this is the Celtics organization choosing to

item.title: Celtics nab Mitchell Robinson from rival Knicks on three-year deal worth nearly $50 million
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent center Mitchell Robinson has agreed to a three-year, $47.4 million deal to sign with the Boston Celtics, with a player option in third season, sources tell ESPN. Robinson departs the Knicks after serving a key role on the historic championship team.

item.title: Mitchell Robinson leaving the Knicks to go to Boston, …
item.description: Ian Begley: Mitchell Robinson leaving the Knicks to go to Boston, source confirms. Robinson's contract - 3-year, $47 million per ESPN - was well, well above anything the Knicks could have offered him while also staying under the 2nd apron. Knicks owner James Dolan said in WFAN interview he didn't want to exceed second apron in team salary this season. Now Knicks will need to replace Robinson

item.title: From a Knick perspective, club has a ton of …
item.description: Ian Begley: From a Knick perspective, club has a ton of appreciation & respect for Mitchell Robinson as he departs for Boston, per source familiar with matter. Robinson's exit is seen a casualty of the situation re: NYK holding team salary under 2nd apron. Robinson, a 2nd-round pick eight years ago who played pivotal role in Knicks developing into NBA champs, leaves indelible mark on franchise. Club could not have come close to Boston's offer to Robinson while also staying under second apron. Kn

item.title: “The analysis of their numbers (and the signing of …
item.description: John-Griner Canady: Brian Windhorst on Jaylen Brown-Celtics “The analysis of their numbers (and the signing of Mitchell Robinson) to me still points to the Celtics wanting to trade Jaylen Brown”

item.title: Shams Charania says the Celtics are “strongly …
item.description: Daniel Donabedian: Shams Charania says the Celtics are “strongly shopping” Jaylen Brown still

item.title: Jaylen Brown traded to Sixers
item.description: Shams Charania: BLOCKBUSTER: The Boston Celtics have agreed to trade Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers for Paul George, two first-round picks and two second-round picks, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: Jaylen Brown is second-fastest Finals MVP to be traded after winning award
item.description: HoopsHype: Jaylen Brown is the second-fastest Finals MVP to be traded after winning the award, behind Dennis Johnson (1979 Finals MVP, traded to Phoenix in 1980).

item.title: Salary cap situation relatively unchanged. George also …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: Boston Celtics after trading Jaylen Brown for Paul George and two first-round picks. Salary cap situation relatively unchanged. George also has a trade bonus that increases his $54.1 million salary to $57.7 million max. They have a surplus of 8 first-round picks and 7 seconds.

item.title: The 2028 pick Boston is getting, or swapping, with the …
item.description: Keith Smith: The 2028 pick Boston is getting, or swapping, with the Sixers is kind of complicated. Boston could get Philadelphia's pick if it lands 1-8. If that pick is 9-30 it's gone from the Sixers in a complicated swap that involves multiple teams. If the Philadelphia pick is 9-30, then Boston could get the most favorable of their own pick and the LA Clippers pick that Philadelphia owns, with the less favorable pick staying with the Sixers. I think that's how it will work out. More to come wh

item.title: Pretty clean swap of contracts
item.description: Bobby Marks: Pretty clean swap of contracts To Boston Paul George 2026-27: $54.1M 2027-28: $56.6M (Player) * Extension eligible To Philadelphia Jaylen Brown 2026-27: $57M 2027-28: $61M 2028-29: $64.9M * Eligible to sign a 1 year extension on 7/26

item.title: By today they were actively shopping full blown …
item.description: Heat Central: "For the Celtics where this had come down to is they tried to trade for Giannis.. They missed out. Ever since then what's been interesting, they didn't stop the conversations there. By today they were actively shopping full blown shopping Jaylen Brown around the league... Jaylen Brown never asked out of Boston. This was all the Celtics" - @ShamsCharania

item.title: Details on 76ers picks to the Celtics, per sources:
item.description: Shams Charania: Details on 76ers picks to the Celtics, per sources: -- 2028 first-round pick that could convert from a first to a swap that is more favorable to Boston -- 2031 unprotected Philadelphia first -- 2028 second round, most favorable of GSW/OKC/MIL -- 2030 second, most favorable of WAS/PORT/PHX

item.title: Multiple league sources confirm that the Celtics have …
item.description: Adam Himmelsbach: Multiple league sources confirm that the Celtics have traded Jaylen Brown to the 76ers for Paul George, two first-round picks and two seconds, ending the Boston tenure of the 5-time All-Star and 2024 Finals MVP.

item.title: He has a $1.8 million trade bonus that will be divided …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: Sixers salary cap situation after trading Paul George and picks for Jaylen Brown. He has a $1.8 million trade bonus that will be divided over the next three years. He will be extension-eligible during the offseason for up to two additional years.

item.title: Before Jaylen, the last player to finish Top 6 in MVP …
item.description: HoopsHype: Jaylen Brown finished No. 6 in MVP voting in 2025-26. Before Jaylen, the last player to finish Top 6 in MVP voting and get traded the following offseason was... Paul George.

item.title: Not a single Jaylen-to-Sixers trade rumor on …
item.description: HoopsHype: FWIW: Not a single Jaylen-to-Sixers trade rumor on HoopsHype's Rumors page EVER.

item.title: Paul George's trade kicker will lift his salary to the …
item.description: One cap note from Boston's side on today's blockbuster: Paul George's trade kicker will lift his salary to the 35-percent max of $57,736,350 for the coming year, unless he waives it.

item.title: Jaylen Brown fell out of favor with Celtics?
item.description: Brown is durable. He’s a three-level scorer. He’s someone who has been an NBA Finals MVP and a champion. He’s also polarizing, thoughtful, well-spoken, outspoken, expensive and had apparently fallen so out of favor with Brad Stevens and the rest of Boston’s front office that he was traded for relative pennies on the dollar.

item.title: This was their super move...They feel really good …
item.description: Bobby Krivitsky: Chris Haynes on what's next for the Celtics: "I think if Boston makes any moves moving forward now, it will be around the edges. This was their super move...They feel really good about how they've positioned themselves. They like the structure of their roster moving forward into next season."

item.title: Thank you for everything you gave to the city of …
item.description: Derrick White: Nothing but love for you JB! 🍀 Thank you for everything you gave to the city of Boston and this franchise! Wishing you nothing but success in the next chapter of your career! 🫡

item.title: Jaylen Brown never asked for a trade,
item.description: Chris Mannix: Jaylen Brown never asked for a trade, sources confirm to @SInow. Brown’s decade-long run—which included six conference finals, two NBA Finals and one championship—comes to a shocking end.

item.title: Maybe they mad Brown showed professionalism by showing …
item.description: Eddie A Johnson: Brown, led a team in Tank Mode to a 56 win season and they move off of him. Maybe they mad Brown showed professionalism by showing up to work and leading a team of unheard of players to playoffs and they wanted high draft picks in supposedly the best draft class in years.

item.title: "Even though Jaylen Brown was talking about himself …
item.description: Fullcourtpass: The Boston Celtics believe Derrick White had a better season than Jaylen Brown last year, per @WindhorstESPN "Even though Jaylen Brown was talking about himself for MVP, the Celtics did not feel like Jaylen had the best season on their team. They felt that Derrick White had a better season.” (h/t @MrBuckBuckNBA)

item.title: "This was not a trade that you would make in a …
item.description: NBA Base: There is speculation around the league that the Celtics’ trade sending Jaylen Brown to the 76ers was made ‘under duress,’ per @WindhorstESPN 😳 "This was not a trade that you would make in a clinical setting... You wouldn't do this trade under these circumstances if everything was equal, that something wasn't equal."

item.title: So, Boston either comes away with a decent pick …
item.description: Keith Smith: For full clarity, here are the various options for the 2028 swap/pick the Celtics got in the trade with the Sixers: 1. If LAC is 1-16 AND PHI is 1-8, then BOS gets the best of LAC/PHI/[lesser of BOS (top-1 protected)/SAS] 2. If LAC is 1-16 and PHI is 9-30, then BOS gets the best of LAC/[lesser of BOS (top-1 protected)/SAS] 3. If LAC is 17-30 and PHI is 1-8, then BOS gets the best of PHI/lesser of BOS (top-1 protected)/SAS] 4. If LAC is 17-30 and PHI is 9-30, then BOS keeps lesser of

item.title: I had the good fortune to cover some real gems in my …
item.description: I had the good fortune to cover some real gems in my 22 years on the Timberwolves beat. Mike Conley is right near the top. I learned something about the game from him with every conversation. He was as gracious with his time as anyone. I understood the team better than ever thanks to him.

item.title: Jaylen Brown was as present in the community as any …
item.description: Bobby Krivitsky: Jaylen Brown was as present in the community as any star you'll find. His efforts through the 7uice Foundation, the Bridge Program, and Boston XChange highlighted the bond he built with the city.

item.title: New York (+850), jumping into third place on the title …
item.description: Sitting firmly in third place in the NBA Finals winner market since the start of the offseason, behind the San Antonio Spurs (+270) and Oklahoma City Thunder (+270), Boston dipped from +650 odds before the trade to +1100 odds after. New York (+850), jumping into third place on the title odds board past the rival Celtics, is now back in the Eastern Conference driver’s seat despite a quiet offseason focused on re-signing role players. Even with the Knicks losing backup center and offensive rebound

item.title: The Celtics trading Brown is a bet that Payton …
item.description: The Celtics trading Brown is a bet that Payton Pritchard is their in-house version of New York Knicks guard Jalen Brunson, a diminutive player who didn't fully show his talents until Luka Dončić was out of the picture. The splits on Pritchard without Brown are intriguing to say the least.

item.title: Celtics agonized for days over the decision to trade Jaylen Brown
item.description: News of the deal sent shockwaves through the NBA and rattled a puzzled Celtics fan base Wednesday. One league source said Boston’s brass agonized for days over the decision to trade Brown but eventually determined that acquiring valuable assets along with a nine-time All-Star in George offered the franchise its best path forward.

item.title: Brown, who led the Celtics to 56 wins with Jayson …
item.description: One league source said Brown, who led the Celtics to 56 wins with Jayson Tatum sidelined for most of the season following an Achilles injury, never requested a trade and had grown frustrated with how the Celtics were handling this situation. The source said Wednesday that it was too early to know how Brown was processing this trade. Brown did not respond to a text message seeking comment.

item.title: But a league source stressed that this was not a …
item.description: George, 36, has two years remaining on his four-year, $211 million contract. He will make $54.1 million next season and has a $56.6 million player option for 2027-28. He has one less year on his contract than Brown, and the move will save Boston about $2.9 million this season. But a league source stressed that this was not a cost-cutting transaction, and there was no directive from ownership to trade Brown.

item.title: The Celtics were 90-36 (.714) in games that Jaylen …
item.description: Tom Haberstroh: One possible reason Brad Stevens made the move he did: The Celtics were 90-36 (.714) in games that Jaylen Brown did NOT play. 36-6 (.857) in last three seasons. Equivalent of a 70-win team.

item.title: "What people are saying to me, Bobby, and this is just …
item.description: Brian Windhorst: "What people are saying to me, Bobby, and this is just the league speculation, is that this was a trade that was made under duress. This was not a trade that you would make in a clinical setting. There is no deadline, it didn't have to do it by the end of July 4th, that you wouldn't do this trade under these circumstances if everything was equal, if something wasn't equal."

item.title: Chris Paul fuels return speculation with Jayson Tatum …
item.description: Chris Paul fuels return speculation with Jayson Tatum workout video

item.title: Celtics originally wanted VJ Edgecombe from the 76ers
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Shams on the Jaylen Brown trade: "Sixers president reached out to the Celtics made offers in recent weeks...the Celtics had such a high bar on a deal. They were thinking if you're gonna trade us Paul George we need 4 firsts, 5 firsts, we need a player like a VJ Edgecombe. That was the bar that was set early. There were other teams around the league that might of stopped conversations or moved on to do other deals but the Sixers stayed with it...they stuck around and at the end o

item.title: Shapiro immediately reacted on X with a message, …
item.description: The political taunt came in immediate response to a breaking report from ESPN’s Shams Charania: “BLOCKBUSTER: The Boston Celtics have agreed to trade Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers for Paul George, two first-round picks and two second-round picks, sources tell ESPN,” Charania tweeted. Shapiro immediately reacted on X with a message, “Welcome to Philly, JB! Sixers get way better and, as a bonus, the Celtics got worse!”

item.title: The analytics-driven front offices believe that Jaylen …
item.description: [MacMahon] The simple fact is, the analytics-driven front offices believe that Jaylen Brown is significantly overpaid, and they don't value him as much as the media does. The media is the one who votes on all those awards.

item.title: Regardless of where you fall on the Jaylen Brown …
item.description: ‪Lev Akabas‬: Regardless of where you fall on the Jaylen Brown debate, it is pretty remarkable that the Boston Celtics were better with him off the court than on the court over his entire 10-year tenure with the team

item.title: Mitchell Robinson says he wanted to stay in New York: I didn't want this to happen but hopefully the truth comes out
item.description: Kris Pursiainen: Mitchell Robinson on Instagram: “I tried brother I didn’t want this to happen hopefully the truth comes out at some point.”

item.title: Critique basketball all you want… but disease is …
item.description: Donovan Mitchell: Critique basketball all you want… but disease is insane… we gotta stop letting people just say whatever… cmon man! NBA Central: NBA sources believe Jaylen Brown thinks he’s the smartest person in the room, per @colincowherd: “I had two NBA sources … two people in the league, one an executive, one a scout, say that Jaylen Brown has — it’s a disease. He suddenly thinks he’s the smartest guy in every room he’s in … You make a lot of money, suddenly you’re absolutely sure, you don’

item.title: TV schedule for Salt Lake City SL has been updated:
item.description: Kevin Chouinard: TV schedule for Salt Lake City SL has been updated: Utah 7/4 UTA 5:00 ET Prime/ESPN U 7/6 OKC 7:00 ET ESPN/Prime 7/7 MEM 7:00 ET Prime/ESPN U Vegas 7/9 SAS 4:30 ET ESPN 2 7/11 BKN 8:00 ET ESPN 7/13 BOS 6:00 ET Prime 7/16 MEM 8:00 ET Prime

item.title: Celtics 'completely shut down' trade calls made over the past weeks on Jayson Tatum
item.description: The Boston Celtics never seriously considered trading Jayson Tatum despite recent inquiries from rival teams, according to ESPN's Shams Charania. Boston instead determined weeks ago that it needed to move on from Jaylen Brown, ultimately sending him to the Philadelphia 76ers for Paul George and two future first round picks. "Whether it was personal situations between the two players or whether it was just the on-court basketball fit, the Celtics had just made the determination that, moving forwa

item.title: Jaylen Brown trade shocks NBA executives
